stem: What is the expectation value of the observable O = Z⊗Z + 0.5·(X⊗X) in the state |00⟩?
code:
from qiskit.quantum_info import Statevector, SparsePauliOp

O = SparsePauliOp.from_list([("ZZ", 1.0), ("XX", 0.5)])
psi = Statevector.from_label("00")
print(psi.expectation_value(O))

options.text: 1.5 — both terms contribute their full coefficient

explanation.correct: |00⟩ is a +1 eigenstate of Z⊗Z, so ⟨ZZ⟩ = 1. X⊗X flips |00⟩ to |11⟩, which is orthogonal to |00⟩, so ⟨XX⟩ = 0. The total is 1·1 + 0.5·0 = 1.0.

distractors.A: Assumes both Pauli terms have expectation 1. ⟨XX⟩ on |00⟩ is 0 (it maps the state to an orthogonal one), so the 0.5 term drops out.
distractors.B: The terms do not cancel; ⟨ZZ⟩ = 1 stands on its own while ⟨XX⟩ = 0.
distractors.C: It is the Z⊗Z term (not X⊗X) that contributes here; X⊗X has zero expectation on |00⟩.
